evidence_rea: if the processing is based on your consent, you have withdrawn your consent
evidence_reg: The Data Subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA grants erasure upon withdrawal of consent when processing is based on consent, but the REG conditions this ground further by requiring that there be 'no other legal ground for the processing'; this changes the substantive scope of when the right applies.

evidence_rea: the data is no longer needed for the purposes for which it was collected
evidence_reg: the personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: Both texts express the same core ground that erasure applies when the data are no longer necessary for the purposes of collection/processing; the wording difference is not clearly meaning-changing on the provided text.

evidence_rea: You have the right to request that we erase your personal data
evidence_reg: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA and REG both concern the data subject's right to erasure, but the REA's omission of 'without undue delay' and use of 'request' instead of 'obtain' do not by themselves establish a direct conflict under the instruction not to treat omission alone as a deviation.
